TXN.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Slide As Iran Peace Efforts Stall— Tesla, ServiceNow, Boeing In Focus (UPDATED)
TXN.description: U.S. stock futures declined on Thursday following Wednesday's record-breaking surge, as peace efforts in the Iran war stalled amid vessel seizures in the Strait of Hormuz. Major indices fell slightly, with the Dow down 0.56%, S&P 500 down 0.38%, and Nasdaq 100 down 0.31%. Key earnings reports from Tesla, Boeing, IBM, and ServiceNow drove individual stock movements, while analysts warn of potential pullbacks as 'Magnificent Seven' firms report earnings.

INBX.headline: Large Drugmakers Eye Inhibrx Cancer Drugs Valued Over $8 Billion, Stock Surges
INBX.description: Inhibrx Biosciences is in early-stage discussions with multiple major pharmaceutical companies regarding a potential joint spin-off of its cancer drug INBRX-106 and another oncology asset, with combined valuations potentially exceeding $9 billion. INBRX-106 is being tested as a standalone therapy and in combination with Merck's Keytruda. The stock surged 39.86% to $116.76 on the news.

GEV.headline: GE Vernova Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
GEV.description: GE Vernova (GEV) shares surged 7.74% to a 52-week high after beating Q1 2026 earnings expectations with adjusted EPS of $2.06 versus $1.88 estimate and revenue of $9.339 billion exceeding consensus. The company reported strong backlog growth of $13 billion sequentially to $163 billion, driven by surging demand in power and electrification segments. GE Vernova raised full-year 2026 guidance for revenue, EBITDA margin, and free cash flow, though the wind segment faced headwinds from tariffs and lo

GEV.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Rise After Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire—Tesla, Boeing, GE Vernova And AT&T In Focus (UPDATED)
GEV.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following Tuesday's decline after President Trump extended the Iran ceasefire. Major indices showed gains with the Dow Jones up 0.56%, S&P 500 up 0.60%, and Nasdaq 100 up 0.76%. Key stocks in focus include Tesla, Boeing, and United Airlines, with analysts noting that crude oil prices will take time to normalize despite the ceasefire extension.

RKLB.headline: 3 Space Stocks Flying Under the Radar and Worth Buying This Month
RKLB.description: While most space stocks have become expensive with valuations exceeding 10x sales, the article identifies three relatively cheaper space stocks worth considering: Redwire (5.8x P/S ratio) with diverse space infrastructure products, Spire Global (below 9x sales) focusing on earth observation with improving cash burn trajectory, and newly public Arxis (9.5x P/S ratio) manufacturing space components. All three offer more affordable entry points compared to peers like Rocket Lab and Firefly Aerospac

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Completes Second Dedicated Launch for Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab successfully launched its second dedicated mission for Japan's JAXA on April 23, 2026, deploying eight spacecraft including educational satellites, ocean-monitoring equipment, and advanced antenna technology. This marks the company's 8th launch of 2026 and 87th overall, reinforcing Electron's reliability as a preferred small launcher for national space agencies.

SMR.headline: NuScale Power Stock Falls 70% in 6 Months. Here's Why the Nuclear Energy Company Faces Headwinds.
SMR.description: NuScale Power's stock has fallen 70% over six months despite recent rallies driven by government interest in space nuclear reactors. While the company has regulatory approval advantages and potential long-term catalysts from space contracts and data center power demands, it remains unprofitable with no commercial reactors built yet. The SMR market is still small and developing, making NuScale a speculative position requiring patience.

ASTS.headline: ASTS Gains As FCC Approval Validates SpaceMobile Technology
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) shares surged 7.37% following FCC approval to deploy and operate up to 248 satellites for direct-to-device cellular broadband coverage in partnership with Verizon, AT&T, and FirstNet. The approval validates the company's technology, though a recent BlueBird 7 satellite was placed in a lower-than-planned orbit and will be de-orbited, with costs expected to be recovered through insurance.

ASTS.headline: AST SpaceMobile Drops 15% After Blue Origin Satellite Mishap
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile's stock plummeted 15% after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket deployed the BlueBird 7 satellite into a lower-than-planned orbit on April 19, forcing de-orbiting of the satellite. Despite this setback and being behind schedule on its 2026 deployment targets, the company maintains strong fundamentals with $3.9 billion in liquidity, $1.2 billion in contracted revenue commitments, and strategic partnerships with major telecom providers.

LUNR.headline: Why Did Intuitive Machines Stock Pop Today?
LUNR.description: Intuitive Machines stock surged 6.2% following the U.S. Air Force's announcement of a $338.8 billion fiscal 2027 budget request, which includes a 124% increase in U.S. Space Force spending. The boost is driven by significant increases in satellite communications (+60% to $6.7B) and missile defense (+70% to $6.8B) spending, areas where Intuitive Machines and its subsidiary Lanteris are positioned to benefit as major contractors.

RDW.headline: 3 Space Stocks Flying Under the Radar and Worth Buying This Month
RDW.description: While most space stocks have become expensive with valuations exceeding 10x sales, the article identifies three relatively cheaper space stocks worth considering: Redwire (5.8x P/S ratio) with diverse space infrastructure products, Spire Global (below 9x sales) focusing on earth observation with improving cash burn trajectory, and newly public Arxis (9.5x P/S ratio) manufacturing space components. All three offer more affordable entry points compared to peers like Rocket Lab and Firefly Aerospac

CAR.headline: Avis Budget Stock: One of History’s Wildest Short Squeezes Explained
CAR.description: Avis Budget (CAR) experienced an extreme short squeeze, surging over 500-600% from $80-100 to above $700 in early April 2026. The squeeze was driven by an extremely small free float (~10 million shares) with two hedge funds owning 71% of the company, combined with short interest at 54-89% of the float. While the parabolic move mirrors historical squeezes like VW 2008 and GME 2021, the company remains fundamentally weak with negative earnings and high debt, suggesting a violent collapse is inevit
